message:
Here's one that feels almost spooky: your family swears the dog trots over to the window and waits a few minutes before you pull into the driveway — like they somehow knew you were on your way. 🐾

There's a fascinating explanation, and it comes down to that incredible nose. Dog-cognition expert Alexandra Horowitz describes how dogs can actually "smell time." When you leave in the morning, your scent fills the house at full strength. All day long it slowly fades, hour by hour. Your dog learns what your scent smells like at the exact moment you usually walk in — so as it drops to that familiar level, they head for the door. Pair that with their internal body clock, which keeps steady time for meals, walks, and naps, and your arrival isn't magic at all… it's just a brilliant little nose doing math. 💛

So you really are the best part of their day — and they can practically smell you coming. 🐶

Does your dog somehow know when you're almost home? Tell me their "welcome home" routine! 🚗
